tools.description: Close a GitHub issue with a closing comment. Use this when work is complete, the issue is no longer relevant, or it's a duplicate. The closing comment should explain the resolution or reason for closing. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 1 issue(s) can be closed. Target: *.

body.description: Closing comment explaining why the issue is being closed and summarizing any resolution, workaround, or conclusion.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

issue_number.description: Issue number to close. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 901 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/901). If omitted, closes the issue that triggered this workflow (requires an issue event trigger).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

state_reason.description: The reason for closing the issue. Use 'COMPLETED' for resolved issues, 'NOT_PLANNED' for issues that won't be addressed, or 'DUPLICATE' for duplicate issues. Defaults to 'COMPLETED'.

tools.name: close_issue

tools.description: Add a comment to an existing GitHub issue, pull request, or discussion. Use this to provide feedback, answer questions, or add information to an existing conversation. For creating new items, use create_issue, create_discussion, or create_pull_request instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 2 comment(s) can be added. Target: *. Supports reply_to_id for discussion threading.

body.description: The comment text in Markdown format. This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 123 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/123). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run. If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). If auto-targeting fails (e.g., for schedule or workflow_dispatch triggers), the tool call will fail with an error.

reply_to_id.description: Node ID of the discussion comment to reply to, enabling threaded discussion comments. When provided, the new comment is posted as a reply to the specified top-level discussion comment. If the given node ID belongs to a nested reply, the handler automatically resolves it to the top-level parent. Only applicable for discussion comments — ignored for issue and pull request comments.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

temporary_id.description: Unique temporary identifier for this comment. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Auto-generated if not provided. The temporary ID is returned in the tool response so you can reference this comment later.

tools.name: add_comment

tools.description: Add labels to an existing GitHub issue or pull request for categorization and filtering. Labels must already exist in the repository. For creating new issues with labels, use create_issue with the labels property instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be added. Target: *.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: Issue or PR number to add labels to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, adds labels to the item that triggered this workflow.

labels.description: Label names to add (e.g., ['bug', 'priority-high']). Labels must exist in the repository. This field is required — omitting it will cause a validation error.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: add_labels

tools.description: Remove labels from an existing GitHub issue or pull request. If a label is not present on the item, it will be silently skipped. Use this to clean up labels that are no longer applicable. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be removed. Target: *.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: Issue or PR number to remove labels from. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, removes labels from the item that triggered this workflow.

labels.description: Label names to remove (e.g., ['bug', 'needs-triage']). Labels that don't exist on the item are silently skipped. At least one label must be provided.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: remove_labels

tools.description: Assign one or more GitHub users to an issue. Use this to delegate work to specific team members. Users must have access to the repository.

assignee.description: Single GitHub username to assign. Use 'assignees' array for multiple users.

assignees.description: GitHub usernames to assign to the issue (e.g., ['octocat', 'mona']). Users must have access to the repository.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

issue_number.description: Issue number to assign users to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 543 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/543). If omitted, assigns to the issue that triggered this workflow.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: assign_to_user

tools.description: Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tool.description: Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Be specific about what functionality is needed.

tools.name: missing_tool

tools.description: Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

message.description: Status or completion message to log. Should explain what was analyzed and the outcome (e.g., 'Code review complete - no issues found', 'Analysis complete - all tests passing').

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: noop

tools.description: Report that data or information needed to complete the task is not available. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because required data, context, or information is missing.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

context.description: Additional context about the missing data or where it should come from (max 256 characters).

data_type.description: Type or description of the missing data or information (max 128 characters). Be specific about what data is needed.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this data is needed to complete the task (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: missing_data

tools.description: Signal that the task could not be completed due to an infrastructure or tool failure (e.g., MCP server crash, missing authentication, inaccessible repository). Use this when required tools or data are unavailable and the task cannot be meaningfully performed. This is distinct from noop (no action needed) — it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running. The workflow framework will treat this as a failure signal even when the agent exits successfully.

details.description: Optional extended details or diagnostic context about the failure (max 65000 characters).

reason.description: A concise explanation of why the task could not be completed (max 1024 characters). Be specific about which tools or resources were unavailable.

tools.name: report_incomplete

tools.description: Post a routing comment @mentioning team owners on the triggering issue; bypasses safe-outputs mention neutralization

message.description: The comment body text without any @mentions or @ symbols

owners.description: Comma-separated GitHub usernames to notify, without the @ prefix (e.g. 'user1, user2, Azure/team-name')

tools.name: mention_owners

annotations.title: Get a specific label from a repository.

tools.description: Get a specific label from a repository.
tools.name: get_label

tools.description: Get information about a specific issue in a GitHub repository.

method.description:
The read operation to perform on a single issue.
Options are:
1. get - Get details of a specific issue.
2. get_comments - Get issue comments.
3. get_sub_issues - Get sub-issues of the issue.
4. get_labels - Get labels assigned to the issue.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: issue_read

tools.description: List supported issue types for repository owner (organization).

owner.description: The organization owner of the repository

tools.name: list_issue_types

tools.description: List issues in a GitHub repository. For pagination, use the 'endCursor' from the previous response's 'pageInfo' in the 'after' parameter.

after.description: Cursor for pagination. Use the endCursor from the previous page's PageInfo for GraphQL APIs.

direction.description: Order direction. If provided, the 'orderBy' also needs to be provided.

orderBy.description: Order issues by field. If provided, the 'direction' also needs to be provided.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

state.description: Filter by state, by default both open and closed issues are returned when not provided

tools.name: list_issues

tools.description: Search for issues in GitHub repositories using issues search syntax already scoped to is:issue

owner.description: Optional repository owner. If provided with repo, only issues for this repository are listed.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

query.description: Search query using GitHub issues search syntax

repo.description: Optional repository name. If provided with owner, only issues for this repository are listed.

sort.description: Sort field by number of matches of categories, defaults to best match

tools.name: search_issues

content.text: {"number":58593,"title":"[ServiceNetworking] Docs: Fill gaps in Azure.Provisioning.ServiceNetworking README after initial release","body":"## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Azure.Provisioning.ServiceNetworking` **Service directory:** `sdk/servicenetworking/Azure.Provisioning.ServiceNetworking/` **Triggered by:** PR #58585 by `@azure-sdk` ## What Changed The `Azure.Provisioning.ServiceNetworking` package was released as `1.0.0-beta.1` with support for four `Microsoft.ServiceNetworking` resources: `TrafficController`, `TrafficControllerFrontend`, `TrafficControllerAssociation`, and `ApplicationGatewayForContainersSecurityPolicy`. After the release, a version bump commit (#58585) was pushed to increment the version to `1.0.0-beta.2 (Unreleased)`. ## Gaps Found - **Empty "Authenticate the Client" section** — `sdk/servicenetworking/Azure.Provisioning.ServiceNetworking/README.md` has an `### Authenticate the Client` heading with no content. Provisioning libraries work declaratively (no runtime authentication needed), but the section should still explain how the generated Bicep/ARM is deployed via `azd` and link to relevant docs. - **Empty "Next steps" section** — `## Next steps` heading exists but is completely blank. It should link to related packages (e.g., `Azure.Provisioning`, `Azure.Provisioning.Network`) and relevant documentation. - **`ApplicationGatewayForContainersSecurityPolicy` not documented** — The CHANGELOG lists this resource as supported in `1.0.0-beta.1`, but the README Examples section only demonstrates `TrafficController`, `TrafficControllerFrontend`, and `TrafficControllerAssociation`. There is no example or even a mention of `ApplicationGatewayForContainersSecurityPolicy`. - **Key concepts section is generic** — It describes declarative provisioning in general but does not name or explain the specific resources this package provides. 📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Modify files **File:** `sdk/servicenetworking/Azure.Provisioning.ServiceNetworking/README.md` — **Edit** 1. **Fill in the "Authenticate the Client" section.** Replace the empty `### Authenticate the Client` section body with content similar to other `Azure.Provisioning.*` packages (e.g., `sdk/servicenetworking/README.md` or `sdk/appservice/Azure.Provisioning.AppService/README.md`). Example content: ```markdown ### Authenticate the Client `Azure.Provisioning.ServiceNetworking` is a declarative provisioning library. It generates Bicep (or ARM) templates that are deployed to Azure using tools such as the [Azure Developer CLI (`azd`)]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted) No SDK authentication credentials are required at provisioning time. ``` 2. **Fill in the "Next steps" section.** Add links to related packages and documentation: ```markdown ## Next steps - Explore [Azure.Provisioning](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/provisioning/Azure.Provisioning/README.md) for the core declarative infrastructure primitives. - Explore [Azure.Provisioning.Network](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/network/Azure.Provisioning.Network/README.md) for virtual network resources often used alongside Traffic Controllers. - Learn about [Application Gateway for Containers]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted) ``` 3. **Expand the "Key concepts" section** to name the supported resources: ```markdown ## Key concepts This library supports the following `Microsoft.ServiceNetworking` resources: - **`TrafficController`** — Represents an Application Gateway for Containers instance. - **`TrafficControllerFrontend`** — A frontend (public IP / hostname) attached to a Traffic Controller. - **`TrafficControllerAssociation`** — Associates a subnet with a Traffic Controller. - **`ApplicationGatewayForContainersSecurityPolicy`** — A security policy applied to an Application Gateway for Containers instance. Each resource can be added to an `Infrastructure` object and rendered into deployable Bicep using `azd` or the Azure Provisioning framework. ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets Add a new snippet demonstrating `ApplicationGatewayForContainersSecurityPolicy`. 1. **Write the sample code** in `sdk/servicenetworking/Azure.Provisioning.ServiceNetworking/tests/BasicServiceNetworkingTests.cs` (or a new `BasicServiceNetworkingSecurityPolicyTests.cs`): ```csharp internal static Trycep CreateSecurityPolicyTest() { return new Trycep().Define( ctx => { #region Snippet:ServiceNetworkingSecurityPolicy Infrastructure infra = new(); TrafficController controller = new(nameof(controller), TrafficController.ResourceVersions.V2025_01_01); infra.Add(controller); ApplicationGatewayForContainersSecurityPolicy securityPolicy = new(nameof(securityPolicy), ApplicationGatewayForContainersSecurityPolicy.ResourceVersions.V2025_01_01) { Parent = controller, }; infra.Add(securityPolicy); #endregion return infra; }); } ``` > **Note:** Verify the correct constructor and property signatures by inspecting the generated source files under `sdk/servicenetworking/Azure.Provisioning.ServiceNetworking/src/Generated/`. Adjust property names as needed to match what was actually generated. 2. **Add the snippet placeholder** to `README.md` in the Examples section, after the existing Traffic Controller example: ````markdown ### Create a Security Policy This example demonstrates how to attach an `ApplicationGatewayForContainersSecurityPolicy` to a Traffic Controller. ```C# Snippet:ServiceNetworkingSecurityPolicy ``` ```` The `Update-Snippets.ps1` script will fill in the code block from the `#region` in the test file. ### Step 3: Verify README structure Current structure assessment: | Section | Status | |---|---| | Getting started / Install the package | ✅ Present | | Getting started / Prerequisites | ✅ Present | | Getting started / Authenticate the Client | ❌ **Empty** — needs content | | Key concepts | ⚠️ Present but generic — expand to list resources | | Examples | ⚠️ Missing `ApplicationGatewayForContainersSecurityPolicy` | | Troubleshooting | ✅ Present | | Next steps | ❌ **Empty** — needs links | | Contributing | ✅ Present | Reference: `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/README.md` for canonical formatting and tone. ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order: 1. `dotnet build sdk/servicenetworking/Azure.Provisioning.ServiceNetworking/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/servicenetworking/Azure.Provisioning.ServiceNetworking/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 servicenetworking` — confirm README was updated with the new snippet 4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 servicenetworking` — regenerate public API listing (no public API changes expected, but run to confirm) 5. `dotnet format sdk/servicenetworking/Azure.Provisioning.ServiceNetworking/` ## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above > [!WARNING] > > ⚠️ Firewall blocked 1 domain > > The following domain was blocked by the firewall during workflow execution: > > - `centralus2.in.applicationinsights.azure.com` > > To allow these domains, add them to the `network.allowed` list in your workflow frontmatter: > > ```yaml > network: > allowed: > - defaults > - "centralus2.in.applicationinsights.azure.com" > ``` > > See [Network Configuration](https://github.github.com/gh-aw/reference/network/) for more information. > > > Generated by [Update Docs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/actions/runs/24828681010/agentic_workflow) · ● 456.7K · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3AAzure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net+is%3Aissue+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+Azure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net%2Fupdate-samples-and-docs%22&type=issues) ","state":"open","html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/58593","user":{"login":"github-actions[bot]","id":41898282,"profile_url":"https://github.com/apps/github-actions","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/in/15368?v=4"},"author_association":"NONE","reactions":{"total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"hooray":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"created_at":"2026-04-23T09:58:41Z","updated_at":"2026-04-23T09:58:41Z"}

{"number":58593,"title":"[ServiceNetworking] Docs: Fill gaps in Azure.Provisioning.ServiceNetworking README after initial release","body":"## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Azure.Provisioning.ServiceNetworking` **Service directory:** `sdk/servicenetworking/Azure.Provisioning.ServiceNetworking/` **Triggered by:** PR #58585 by `@azure-sdk` ## What Changed The `Azure.Provisioning.ServiceNetworking` package was released as `1.0.0-beta.1` with support for four `Microsoft.ServiceNetworking` resources: `TrafficController`, `TrafficControllerFrontend`, `TrafficControllerAssociation`, and `ApplicationGatewayForContainersSecurityPolicy`. After the release, a version bump commit (#58585) was pushed to increment the version to `1.0.0-beta.2 (Unreleased)`. ## Gaps Found - **Empty "Authenticate the Client" section** — `sdk/servicenetworking/Azure.Provisioning.ServiceNetworking/README.md` has an `### Authenticate the Client` heading with no content. Provisioning libraries work declaratively (no runtime authentication needed), but the section should still explain how the generated Bicep/ARM is deployed via `azd` and link to relevant docs. - **Empty "Next steps" section** — `## Next steps` heading exists but is completely blank. It should link to related packages (e.g., `Azure.Provisioning`, `Azure.Provisioning.Network`) and relevant documentation. - **`ApplicationGatewayForContainersSecurityPolicy` not documented** — The CHANGELOG lists this resource as supported in `1.0.0-beta.1`, but the README Examples section only demonstrates `TrafficController`, `TrafficControllerFrontend`, and `TrafficControllerAssociation`. There is no example or even a mention of `ApplicationGatewayForContainersSecurityPolicy`. - **Key concepts section is generic** — It describes declarative provisioning in general but does not name or explain the specific resources this package provides. 📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Modify files **File:** `sdk/servicenetworking/Azure.Provisioning.ServiceNetworking/README.md` — **Edit** 1. **Fill in the "Authenticate the Client" section.** Replace the empty `### Authenticate the Client` section body with content similar to other `Azure.Provisioning.*` packages (e.g., `sdk/servicenetworking/README.md` or `sdk/appservice/Azure.Provisioning.AppService/README.md`). Example content: ```markdown ### Authenticate the Client `Azure.Provisioning.ServiceNetworking` is a declarative provisioning library. It generates Bicep (or ARM) templates that are deployed to Azure using tools such as the [Azure Developer CLI (`azd`)]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted) No SDK authentication credentials are required at provisioning time. ``` 2. **Fill in the "Next steps" section.** Add links to related packages and documentation: ```markdown ## Next steps - Explore [Azure.Provisioning](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/provisioning/Azure.Provisioning/README.md) for the core declarative infrastructure primitives. - Explore [Azure.Provisioning.Network](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/network/Azure.Provisioning.Network/README.md) for virtual network resources often used alongside Traffic Controllers. - Learn about [Application Gateway for Containers]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted) ``` 3. **Expand the "Key concepts" section** to name the supported resources: ```markdown ## Key concepts This library supports the following `Microsoft.ServiceNetworking` resources: - **`TrafficController`** — Represents an Application Gateway for Containers instance. - **`TrafficControllerFrontend`** — A frontend (public IP / hostname) attached to a Traffic Controller. - **`TrafficControllerAssociation`** — Associates a subnet with a Traffic Controller. - **`ApplicationGatewayForContainersSecurityPolicy`** — A security policy applied to an Application Gateway for Containers instance. Each resource can be added to an `Infrastructure` object and rendered into deployable Bicep using `azd` or the Azure Provisioning framework. ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets Add a new snippet demonstrating `ApplicationGatewayForContainersSecurityPolicy`. 1. **Write the sample code** in `sdk/servicenetworking/Azure.Provisioning.ServiceNetworking/tests/BasicServiceNetworkingTests.cs` (or a new `BasicServiceNetworkingSecurityPolicyTests.cs`): ```csharp internal static Trycep CreateSecurityPolicyTest() { return new Trycep().Define( ctx => { #region Snippet:ServiceNetworkingSecurityPolicy Infrastructure infra = new(); TrafficController controller = new(nameof(controller), TrafficController.ResourceVersions.V2025_01_01); infra.Add(controller); ApplicationGatewayForContainersSecurityPolicy securityPolicy = new(nameof(securityPolicy), ApplicationGatewayForContainersSecurityPolicy.ResourceVersions.V2025_01_01) { Parent = controller, }; infra.Add(securityPolicy); #endregion return infra; }); } ``` > **Note:** Verify the correct constructor and property signatures by inspecting the generated source files under `sdk/servicenetworking/Azure.Provisioning.ServiceNetworking/src/Generated/`. Adjust property names as needed to match what was actually generated. 2. **Add the snippet placeholder** to `README.md` in the Examples section, after the existing Traffic Controller example: ````markdown ### Create a Security Policy This example demonstrates how to attach an `ApplicationGatewayForContainersSecurityPolicy` to a Traffic Controller. ```C# Snippet:ServiceNetworkingSecurityPolicy ``` ```` The `Update-Snippets.ps1` script will fill in the code block from the `#region` in the test file. ### Step 3: Verify README structure Current structure assessment: | Section | Status | |---|---| | Getting started / Install the package | ✅ Present | | Getting started / Prerequisites | ✅ Present | | Getting started / Authenticate the Client | ❌ **Empty** — needs content | | Key concepts | ⚠️ Present but generic — expand to list resources | | Examples | ⚠️ Missing `ApplicationGatewayForContainersSecurityPolicy` | | Troubleshooting | ✅ Present | | Next steps | ❌ **Empty** — needs links | | Contributing | ✅ Present | Reference: `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/README.md` for canonical formatting and tone. ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order: 1. `dotnet build sdk/servicenetworking/Azure.Provisioning.ServiceNetworking/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/servicenetworking/Azure.Provisioning.ServiceNetworking/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 servicenetworking` — confirm README was updated with the new snippet 4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 servicenetworking` — regenerate public API listing (no public API changes expected, but run to confirm) 5. `dotnet format sdk/servicenetworking/Azure.Provisioning.ServiceNetworking/` ## {"total_count":1,"incomplete_results":false,"items":[{"id":3292095671,"number":51705,"state":"closed","state_reason":"completed","locked":true,"title":"CosmosDBAccountCapability to implement IEquatable<CosmosDBAccountCapability>","body":"## Background
This is a "sub-issue" to one raised in the Aspire repo: [Upgrading to 9.4 causes duplicate capabilities in Cosmos DB and fails deployment](https://github.com/dotnet/aspire/issues/10808).

## Request
It would be great if `CosmosDBAccountCapability` could implement `IEquatable<CosmosDBAccountCapability>`. ## 🎯 Agentic Issue Triage — Needs Manual Review

<details>
<summary>🏷️ Label Decision</summary>

- **Candidate labels considered:**
  - Category `Provisioning` (#ffeb77) — the package `Azure.Provisioning.ServiceNetworking` clearly matches the Provisioning category.
  - Service label for `ServiceNetworking` — checked the repository for labels named `ServiceNetworking` and `Service Networking`; neither exists. No CODEOWNERS `# ServiceLabel:` entry references a ServiceNetworking service label. No quality reference issues (with `customer-reported` + `issue-addressed` + exactly 1 category + exactly 1 service label) were found for this service.
- **Confidence blockers:** Cannot identify an existing service label (color #e99695) for the `ServiceNetworking` service. A category+service label pair requires both labels to exist in the repository; applying a category label without a matching service label is not valid.
- **Outcome:** Applied `needs-triage` — could not confidently predict labels (missing service label).
</details>

<details>
